summaryText: Guest Speaker Scott Williams opened with heartfelt appreciation for Impact Church and its leadership, noting the powerful work of God in the community. He connected his message to Pastor PT's previous teaching on the wilderness, emphasizing that God guides us through difficult seasons. The core message centered on Luke 5:1-11, where Jesus instructs Simon Peter to let down his nets after a fruitless night of fishing. Pastor Scott used this story to teach that hearing and obeying God requires listening with our eyes, ears, and heart. He explained that we must 'listen with our eyes' by paying attention to the details God shows us and preparing faithfully, even in disappointment. We must 'listen with our ears' by obeying God's gentle nudges and stepping out of our comfort zones into deeper faith. Finally, we must 'listen with our heart' by surrendering our insecurities and fully trusting God's call. The sermon concluded with a powerful illustration from a man battling cancer, who learned through fasting that spiritual healing comes from digging deeper into relationship with God. The overarching call was for listeners to go deeper in their faith, trust God's voice over other influences, and experience the breakthrough that comes from obedience.
mainPoints: Listen with your eyes: Pay attention to what God is showing you in your situation and prepare faithfully, knowing that favor finds the faithful.
Listen with your ears: Obey God's gentle nudges and step out of your comfort zone into deeper commitment, trusting that His instructions lead to breakthrough.
Listen with your heart: Surrender your insecurities and fully trust God's call, understanding that true spiritual healing and purpose come from a deep, surrendered relationship with Him.

versesMentioned.text: One day as Jesus was standing by the Lake of Gennesaret, the people were crowding around him and listening to the word of God. He saw at the water’s edge two boats, left there by the fishermen, who were washing their nets. He got into one of the boats, the one belonging to Simon, and asked him to put out a little from shore. Then he sat down and taught the people from the boat. When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, 'Put out into deep water, and let down the nets for a catch.' Simon answered, 'Master, we’ve worked hard all night and haven’t caught anything. But because you say so, I will let down the nets.' When they had done so, they caught such a large number of fish that their nets began to break. So they signaled their partners in the other boat to come and help them, and they came and filled both boats so full that they began to sink. When Simon Peter saw this, he fell at Jesus’ knees and said, 'Go away from me, Lord; I am a sinful man!' For he and all his companions were astonished at the catch of fish they had taken, and so were James and John, the sons of Zebedee, Simon’s partners. Then Jesus said to Simon, 'Don’t be afraid; from now on you will fish for people.' So they pulled their boats up on shore, left everything and followed him.
The eye is the window to the soul.

All the counsel you have received has only worn you out! Let your astrologers come forward, those stargazers who make predictions month by month, let them save you from what is coming upon you. Surely they are like stubble; the fire will burn them up. They cannot even save themselves from the power of the flame. Here are no coals to warm anyone; here is no fire to sit by.

Monday.scripture: Luke 5:4-5 - '...But because you say so, I will let down the nets.'
Monday.action: Identify one thing God has been prompting you to do that feels outside your comfort zone. Commit to taking the first step today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, give me the courage to obey You simply because You say so. Help me trust Your voice over my doubts.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 30:17 - 'The eye is the window to the soul.'
Tuesday.action: Spend 10 minutes in quiet reflection. Ask God to show you one 'blind spot' or pattern in your life He wants to address. Write it down.
Tuesday.prayer: God, open my eyes to see what You are showing me. Help me to be honest about the areas where I need to prepare and change.

Wednesday.scripture: Luke 5:8 - 'Go away from me, Lord; I am a sinful man!'
Wednesday.action: Reflect on an insecurity that leads you to sin (e.g., people-pleasing, fear). Confess it to God and choose one act of trust in Him instead.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, I surrender my insecurities to You. Heal my heart and help me find my worth and confidence in You alone.

Thursday.scripture: Luke 5:10 - 'Don’t be afraid; from now on you will fish for people.'
Thursday.action: Encourage one person today. Share a word of hope or a simple testimony of what God has done for you.
Thursday.prayer: Father, replace my fear with Your purpose. Use me to be a blessing and point someone to You today.

Friday.scripture: Isaiah 47:13-14 - '...the fire will burn them up. They cannot even save themselves...'
Friday.focus: Trusting God's word over other influences.
Friday.action: Evaluate one source of advice or influence in your life (e.g., media, horoscopes). Decide to replace that time with reading Scripture or prayer.
Friday.prayer: Lord, I choose to trust Your Word above all else. Purify my heart and mind from any influence that does not honor You.

Saturday.scripture: Luke 5:11 - 'So they pulled their boats up on shore, left everything and followed him.'
Saturday.action: Prepare for worship tomorrow. Decide on one area of your life (finances, service, relationships) where you will 'go deeper' and fully follow Christ.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, I leave my old ways behind. I choose to follow You with my whole heart. Prepare me to worship and hear from You tomorrow.
